subject: Ecology
title: Welwitschia: A Living Fossil
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Welwitschia: A Living Fossil.

hook.text: Welwitschia mirabilis has only two leaves—and they grow continuously for over 1,000 years.

buildup.text: Found only in the Namib Desert, this plant looks like a pile of tattered leather straps.

discovery.text: Its leaves absorb fog moisture through millions of stomata on both upper and lower surfaces.

twist.text: Welwitschia is a gymnosperm—more closely related to pines than to flowering plants.

climax.text: Some living specimens may be over 2,000 years old, making them among Earth's oldest organisms.

punchline.text: Two leaves and 2,000 years. Simplicity wins.

quiz.question: How many leaves does Welwitschia produce in its lifetime?
